mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) over the past 12 months signals ongoing economic expansion with improving capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days. The signal is always active but its magnitude varies with the steepness of the production trend.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production correlates with GDP growth historically
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of INDPRO (12-month change) on 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 or the correlation turns negative for any consecutive 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.2% from a low base, it signals the onset of labor market weakness that historically precedes economic downturns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:Sahm rule threshold historically at 0.5 pp rise from trough, here simplified to level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion when UNRATE is at cyclical lows
failure_condition: Fewer than 70% of triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns, or the average return in triggered instances is not statistically lower than baseline at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline simultaneously with nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over a 3-month period, it indicates a synchronized weakening of consumer demand and labor income that depresses corporate revenues and forward earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Joint declines in consumption and employment precede recessions
failure_condition: The forward 60-day return after 3-month joint declines is positive more than 50% of the time, or the signal's rank IC with the interaction term is below 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime defined by the federal funds rate exceeding 3.0%, any further increase in FEDFUNDS tightens financial conditions more aggressively and depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is only active when the regime condition is met.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical hiking cycles compress equity multiples
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, the correlation between weekly changes in FEDFUNDS and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not negative, or the regime-conditional Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth is the most comprehensive measure of aggregate economic output; sustained positive year-over-year growth supports corporate earnings and lifts S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal is always on, as a growing economy broadly benefits equities.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth and equity returns are positively correlated at quarterly frequency
failure_condition: The quarterly correlation of GDPC1 year-over-year change with subsequent 60-day SPX returns is negative over any 10-year period, or the signal fails to produce positive average returns when GDP growth is above trend.

mechanism_narrative: When the 12-month moving average of CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.0%, it signals overheating that compels the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and lowering equity valuations. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical inflation cross above 3% often precedes market drawdowns
failure_condition: More than 50% of triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns, or the average return in triggers is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) both post concurrent monthly increases over 3 consecutive months, it confirms synchronized strength in labor demand and manufacturing output, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint signal amplifies beyond the sum of individual effects.
evidence_refs: fact:Concurrent expansion in labor and industry typically coincides with bull markets
failure_condition: The interaction term (concurrent 3-month increases) yields forward returns not significantly above zero, or the signal's rank IC is below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime (UNRATE < 4.0%), a further one-month decline in UNRATE signals an excessively tight labor market that compresses profit margins via wage pressures and prompts preemptive Fed tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market prices in rate hike fears.
evidence_refs: fact:Very low unemployment historically correlates with late-cycle vulnerabilities
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.0% and declining, the median 5-day forward return is positive, or the Sharpe ratio of the conditional strategy is worse than that of the unconditional long equity position.
